name: Hautes-Pyrenees Road Info - Stage 6 Tourmalet and Gavarnie-Gedre
summary: Official Hautes-Pyrenees road-info site already has a Tour de France 2026 flash item for July 8-9.
whyItMatters: This is the key official road-closure and pass-status source for the Tourmalet viewing day.
pros: Explicit Tour de France 2026 flash item for July 8 and 9
Site covers road restrictions, conditions, webcams, mountain passes, and special events
Departmental article confirms Stage 6 crosses Col d'Aspin and Col du Tourmalet before Gavarnie-Gedre
cons: The linked departmental order currently appears to be a 2025 PDF, so the 2026 order still needs follow-up
Road-closure timing by direction/vehicle type is not yet captured in this JSON
Mountain access rules may change close to race day

logistics.opening_hours: Road information updates continuously; verify on July 8 evening and July 9 morning
logistics.distance_from_base: Arreau to Tourmalet access depends on selected side and closures
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Directly relevant to Col du Tourmalet viewing
logistics.transit: Unknown; check local shuttles or restrictions if announced
logistics.parking: Unknown; wait for 2026 departmental order and local commune notices
logistics.road_closure_notes: Official Stage 6 timetable lists Col du Tourmalet at 16:30 / 16:43 / 16:58 for race pace estimates; road closures will likely start much earlier.

timing.best_visit_window: Decide Tourmalet access plan only after the 2026 departmental order is published

sources.title: InfoRoute65 - Departement des Hautes-Pyrenees
sources.note: Official road-info site with a 'Tour de France 2026 - 8 et 9 juillet 2026' flash item and road-restriction resources.

sources.title: Departement des Hautes-Pyrenees - Tour de France 2026: deux etapes spectaculaires
sources.note: Official departmental article confirming Stage 6 crosses Col d'Aspin and Col du Tourmalet before Gavarnie-Gedre.

sources.title: Tour de France Stage 6 - Pau > Gavarnie-Gedre
sources.note: Official ASO stage page with Tourmalet passage timing and route context.

open_questions: When will the 2026 Hautes-Pyrenees departmental road order PDF be published?
Will access from the Arreau / Sainte-Marie-de-Campan side be closed earlier than access from the Bareges side?
Are any official spectator parking, shuttle, or bike-up rules planned for Tourmalet?
